sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 37, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves active object right 2px within container (VERIFIED step 3)', 'LEFT': 'Moves active object left 2px within container (VERIFIED step 4)', 'UP': "Moves up within container if space exists. Swaps with divider block only at specific 'portal' position (cols 10-11, rows 40-41) (VERIFIED steps 5,13,17)", 'DOWN': 'Moves down within container if space exists. Swaps with divider block at portal position (VERIFIED step 6)', 'MOUSE': 'Does NOT change active object selection (VERIFIED step 9)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'green_pixel': 'Active player object, 2x2, always the controlled entity', 'divider_blocks': 'White 2x2 blocks at col 32-33, rows 10-50 in vertical line', 'gray_containers': 'Irregular gray boxes on left panel holding colored pixels', 'white_containers': 'White frames on right panel containing colored shapes (red and blue)', 'colored_pixels': 'Small 2x2 objects inside containers: yellow, blue, red, dark red, green'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'White horizontal bar at row 63, shrinks as timer/progress indicator', 'divider': 'Vertical line of white 2x2 blocks at col 32-33'}
Current level:
- World model: Two-panel layout. Left (charcoal bg) has gray containers with colored 2x2 pixels. Right (black bg) has white containers with red/blue shapes. Green pixel is the active object. Container has narrow part (cols 8-11) and wide part (cols 8-13). UP/DOWN in narrow part at rows 40-41 swaps with divider blocks. UP/DOWN in wide part moves within container. UP at cols 8-9 or cols 10-11 rows 38-39 does nothing. Green currently at [[38,10],[39,11]].
- Goal model: Move green pixel through divider to right side to match targets in white containers. The 'portal' for swapping with divider is at cols 10-11, rows 40-41.
- Recent findings: ['UP swap with divider works only at cols 10-11, rows 40-41', 'UP at cols 10-11 rows 38-39 does NOT swap (step 17)', 'UP at cols 8-9 does NOT swap (step 16)', 'UP at cols 12-13 moves within container (step 12)']
- Open questions: ["What is the exact 'portal' position for divider swap?", 'How to move green pixel to right side after swapping?', 'What is the target configuration?']
- Plan: Move green DOWN to rows 40-41, keep at cols 10-11, then UP to swap with divider. From divider, try RIGHT to move to right side.
- Strategy log: ['Tried UP from various positions in narrow container part - only rows 40-41 at cols 10-11 triggers swap']
- Failed probes: ['MOUSE(20,24) on yellow pixel: no selection change (step 9)', 'UP at cols 8-9 rows 38-39: no change (step 16)', 'UP at cols 10-11 rows 38-39: no change (step 17)', 'RIGHT at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no movement (step 13)', 'UP at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no change (step 12)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model.

Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
UP at cols 12-13, rows 38-39 didn't work (board changed: False). This confirms that UP is blocked at the top of the container from any position.
Let me think about this differently. The container has:
- Narrow top: rows 38-39, cols 10-13
- Wide bottom: rows 40-43, cols 8-13
Green is at cols 12-13, rows 38-39. UP is blocked. DOWN should work to move into the wide part.
Let me try DOWN to move to rows 40-41. Then I can explore the wide part of the container.
But wait, I already know DOWN works within the container. The question is how to EXIT the container and reach other containers or the right side.
Let me think about the blue checkerboard pattern at rows 34-37, cols 8-11. This pattern is between the middle container (rows 30-33) and bottom container (rows 38-43). The pattern has blue pixels at:
- Row 34: cols 9, 11
- Row 35: cols 8, 10
- Row 36: cols 9, 11
- Row 37: cols 8, 10
This is a checkerboard pattern. It's 4 rows tall and 4 columns wide (cols 8-11). The pattern might be a "bridge" that allows green to move between containers.
But how does green enter the pattern? The pattern is at rows 34-37, and the bottom container starts at row 38. If green could move UP from rows 38-39, it would reach rows 36-37, which is part of the pattern. But UP is blocked.
Wait, maybe I need to look at this from a different angle. What if the containers are not "containers" in the sense of enclosing green, but rather "slots" where colored pixels can be placed? And green is a "cursor" that moves between slots to select which colored pixel to activate?
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me move green DOWN to the bottom of the container, then RIGHT to the right edge, and see if there's an exit there. The container at rows 40-43 has gray at cols 8-13. If green moves RIGHT from cols 12-13, it would go to cols 14-15, which is outside the container (charcoal background). Maybe there's an exit there?
Actually, let me first try DOWN to move into the wide part of the container.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# UP at cols 12-13 rows 38-39 blocked. Let me try DOWN to enter wide part, then explore
action(['DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
green = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'][0]
print(f"Green after DOWN: {green['boundary']}")

# Try DOWN again to go further down
action(['DOWN'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'][0]
print(f"Green after 2nd DOWN: {green['boundary']}")

# Try RIGHT to see if we can exit the container on the right
action(['RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'][0]
print(f"Green after RIGHT: {green['boundary']}")

# Try RIGHT again
action(['RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'][0]
print(f"Green after 2nd RIGHT: {green['boundary']}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

Blue checkerboard pattern at rows 34-37, cols 8-11. Green at [[38,12],[39,13]].", "goal_model": "Move green to right side to match shapes", "recent_findings": ["UP blocked at container top from all positions", "Container narrow top: rows 38-39 cols 10-13; wide bottom: rows 40-43 cols 8-13"], "open_questions": ["How to exit container? How to reach right side?"], "current_plan": "Move DOWN into wide part, then RIGHT to see if there's an exit on the right edge", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["UP from cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no move (step 37)"]}}
</parameter>
